rationale_for_baseline_tier: With a demonstrably patent airway and a benign bilateral lacy oral lesion, this is a panic attack over an incidental finding — low acuity, managed with reassurance, breathing coaching, and routine dental/GP confirmation. The only escalations are for true airway compromise (absent) or a suspicious solitary lesion.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Noisy/whistling breathing, lip/tongue swelling, or inability to swallow liquids
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate a true airway emergency/anaphylaxis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop noisy/whistling breathing, your lips or tongue swell, or you can't swallow liquids, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Partner can help her settle and disengage from mouth-checking.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your airway is open — you can talk and swallow, so your throat isn't closing. The white lacy lines are a benign mouth condition (reticular oral lichen planus), not cancer, and have likely been there for months. Put the torch away, breathe slowly, and see your dentist or GP within a week to confirm. Call 000 only if you get noisy breathing, lip/tongue swelling, or can't swallow liquids.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Dentist/GP within 1 week; 000 for airway red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No emergency care needed unless an airway red flag appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a cancer-fear panic attack over incidental reticular oral lichen planus; airway patency confirmed; benign lesion pattern; reassurance and breathing advice given; routine dental/GP confirmation and health-anxiety support recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without checking airway signs or the lesion pattern, or with no safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A rare true airway problem or a genuinely suspicious solitary lesion is missed because it was never screened.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a patent airway, or urgent oncology work-up for benign bilateral striae, is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform a formal oral examination remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Use demonstrable airway signs and the bilateral lacy pattern to reassure, with routine confirmation and airway/suspicious-lesion safety-nets.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate a patent airway to ED, or reinforce the cancer fear.
